steps.description: C(n,r): descending product of r terms / r!

4! arrangements of the block + 3 others; ×2 for internal AB or BA order

steps.description: Merge A and B into one unit → 4 objects total

steps.description: Within the block, A or B can be first: ×2. 24×2=48

question.latex: \text{How many diagonals does a hexagon have?}
question.plain: How many diagonals does a hexagon (6 sides) have?

palette.description: Total line segments between any 2 of 6 vertices
Subtract the 6 sides to keep only diagonals

C(6,2)=15: every pair of 6 vertices gives a segment

question.plain: What is the coefficient of x²y³ in the expansion of (x+y)⁵?

palette.description: Binomial theorem: coeff of x^r·y^(n−r) in (x+y)^n is C(n,r)

steps.description: Binomial theorem: expand (x+y)^n as sum of C(n,k)x^k y^(n-k)
Set k=2 (power of x): coefficient = C(5,2)

question.latex: \text{Min. people to guarantee 2 share a birth month?}
question.plain: What is the minimum number of people needed to guarantee that at least 2 share a birth month?

palette.description: Pigeonhole: n+1 people for n months. 12 months → 13 people

steps.description: Identify the 'holes': one for each month

steps.fromLatex: \text{Person 13 must share a month with someone}
steps.description: Pigeonhole principle: 13 people into 12 months → at least one month has 2

question.latex: \text{Choose 5 from 12, but A and B must NOT both be chosen}
question.plain: How many ways to choose 5 from 12 people if two specific people (A and B) cannot both be selected?

palette.description: Cases with both A and B: 2 spots taken, choose 3 from remaining 10

steps.fromLatex: \text{Bad cases (A and B both in): choose 3 more from remaining 10}

question.plain: In how many ways can 6 students be divided into 2 equal groups of 3 (the groups have no label/order)?

palette.description: Choose 3 for first group; other 3 form second group automatically
Divide by 2! since the groups are unlabeled — swapping gives the same partition

steps.fromLatex: \text{Label the groups A and B temporarily}
steps.description: C(6,3)=20: ways to put 3 specific people into group A
Groups are unlabeled: every partition counted twice (A,B is same as B,A)
10 distinct ways to split into 2 groups of 3

question.plain: Evaluate C(2,2)+C(3,2)+C(4,2)+C(5,2)+C(6,2) using the hockey stick identity

palette.description: Hockey stick: Σ C(r,k) for r=k..n equals C(n+1,k+1). Here k=2, n=6 → C(7,3)

steps.description: Pattern: each C(r,2) on the diagonal; sum = C(7,3)
Hockey stick identity: sum of diagonal = one cell lower-right

question.latex: \text{4 distinct balls into 3 distinct boxes — how many ways?}
question.plain: How many ways can 4 distinct balls be placed into 3 distinct boxes?

palette.description: Each of 4 balls independently goes into any of 3 boxes: 3 choices per ball

steps.description: FCP: 3 choices for ball 1, 3 for ball 2, etc.

question.latex: \text{4 items into 2 boxes, both boxes non-empty — how many ways?}
question.plain: How many ways can 4 distinct items be placed into 2 distinct boxes so that neither box is empty?

palette.description: Bad cases: all 4 in box 1 or all 4 in box 2 — 2 ways

steps.description: All in box 1 or all in box 2 — exactly 2 bad cases

question.latex: \text{Min. people to guarantee 3 share a birth month?}
question.plain: How many people must be in a room to guarantee that at least 3 share the same birth month?

palette.description: 25 people guarantee at least one month with ≥ 3 people

steps.fromLatex: \text{Worst case: spread people as evenly as possible}
steps.toLatex: \text{12 months: at most 2 per month with 24 people}
steps.description: With 24 people, you could have exactly 2 per month — no guarantee of 3

steps.fromLatex: \text{25th person must go into a month that already has 2}
steps.description: Generalized pigeonhole: with 2×12+1=25 people, some hole has ≥3

question.latex: \text{Arrange A,B,C,D,E: A not first, E not last — how many?}
question.plain: Arrangements of A,B,C,D,E where A is NOT in position 1 AND E is NOT in position 5

steps.description: A in pos 1: fix A, arrange rest=4!=24. E in pos 5: 4!=24. Both: fix A+E, arrange 3=3!=6

steps.description: Binomial theorem with a=x, b=2y. Set k=2 for x²
